slides.title: State 🟢
slides.content:
A Class Blueprint is defined by two things:
1. State (What does it know?)
2. Behavior (What can it do?)

We define 'State' using **Attributes** (also known as Instance Variables or Fields).

slides.title: Variables inside Classes 🏷️
slides.content:
These are simply standard variables declared directly inside the `class` block.

Every time you instantiate an Object using `new`, that specific Object gets its very own, separate copy of those variables.

quizData.question: Which symbol is used to access or modify a specific Object's internal attributes?
quizData.explanation: The Dot notation (`myDog.name`) means 'go inside the myDog object and find the variable called name'.

slides.title: Independent Objects 👯
slides.content:
The power of OOP is that objects are completely independent.

If you have two `Dog` objects, changing the age of one does NOT change the age of the other. They are entirely separated in memory.

quizData.question: If you build two houses from the exact same blueprint, painting the first house Blue automatically paints the second house Blue as well.
quizData.explanation: The blueprint establishes that houses CAN have a color. But once built, every individual house Object holds its own data. Painting one doesn't paint the other.
